intent: Define the SecurityRecipes agentic incident response pack: a machine-readable response model for failures in secure context retrieval, MCP tool execution, agent identity, handoffs, memory, skills, eval drift, and high-impact autonomous actions.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, SOC, Product Security, GRC, trust and safety, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI incident response and forensics for the secure context layer
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can help an enterprise detect, contain, reconstruct, replay, and disclose agentic incidents using the same MCP-readable evidence packs that authorize production agent work.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI programs are moving from pilots into production tool use. Buyers need response evidence for prompt injection, context poisoning, tool misuse, over-broad MCP authorization, token passthrough, agent handoff leakage, persistent memory drift, skill supply-chain compromise, and high-impact autonomy before approving broad rollout.

standards_alignment.name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 Incident Response Recommendations
standards_alignment.coverage: Incident response as a CSF 2.0 profile covering preparation, detection, response, recovery, lessons learned, and cybersecurity risk management integration.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
standards_alignment.coverage: Agent authentication, identity infrastructure, secure multi-agent interaction, open protocols, and security evaluations for trusted interoperable agents.

standards_alignment.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
standards_alignment.coverage: Indirect prompt injection, data poisoning, misaligned actions, measurement, and deployment interventions that constrain and monitor agent access.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Critical risks for autonomous and agentic AI systems that plan, act, make decisions, use tools, communicate with other agents, and consume mutable context.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Protected resource metadata, OAuth 2.1, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, scope handling, token theft, confused deputy prevention, and token privilege restriction.

standards_alignment.name: Joint Guidance on Deploying AI Systems Securely
standards_alignment.coverage: Operational controls to protect, detect, and respond to malicious activity against AI systems, related data, and services.

conditions: confirmed secret, credential, regulated data, customer data, or payment/production control-plane exposure outside the approved boundary
high-impact autonomous action executed without linked approval or risk acceptance
agent identity, MCP token, or remote-agent handoff used after revocation
probable context poisoning, token passthrough, authorization confused deputy, tool misuse, or cross-tenant context movement with incomplete exposure proof
customer impact is possible or unbounded while forensic evidence is still being correlated
agent behavior, MCP decision, memory replay, skill update, or handoff outcome cannot be explained by current receipts and source hashes
evidence exists but is incomplete, stale, unsigned, or missing a correlation id
single suspicious signal with no external side effect, no sensitive data movement, and complete run evidence

response_phases.title: Detect and classify
response_phases.objective: Normalize raw telemetry into an agentic incident class, severity signal, affected workflow, run receipt, identity, context package, MCP namespace, and correlation id.

response_phases.title: Contain and revoke
response_phases.objective: Pause the run, kill the session when required, revoke delegated identity, freeze MCP scopes, block unsafe context, and quarantine remote-agent or skill surfaces.

response_phases.title: Preserve forensics
response_phases.objective: Snapshot source hashes, context package hashes, prompts, tool decisions, authorization claims, egress decisions, handoff decisions, receipts, eval outcomes, and reviewer approvals without mutating evidence.

response_phases.title: Eradicate and patch
response_phases.objective: Remove poisoned context, tighten MCP authorization, disable unsafe skills or memory entries, update evals, and add deterministic regression cases before restoring autonomy.

response_phases.title: Replay and recertify
response_phases.objective: Replay the incident as an eval, measurement probe, red-team drill, and catastrophic-risk scenario when applicable before lifting holds or promoting workflow readiness.

response_phases.title: Disclose and learn
response_phases.objective: Produce customer, executive, GRC, and trust-center readouts with exact evidence paths, blast-radius decisions, timeline, containment proof, and recurrence controls.

incident_classes.title: Context poisoning or prompt-injection intake
kill_signals: critical poisoning finding outside approved adversarial-example roots
retrieved context contains hidden instructions, tool override text, encoded exfiltration payload, or approval bypass instructions

incident_classes.title: MCP tool misuse or unsafe side effect
kill_signals: tool call attempts a namespace, access mode, path, branch, tenant, or side effect outside generated policy
agent repeats denied or held tool calls after policy response

incident_classes.title: Agent identity or delegated authority abuse
kill_signals: identity is missing, expired, over-scoped, used after revocation, or acting outside workflow contract
implicit user token is used where a scoped non-human identity is required

incident_classes.title: MCP authorization confused deputy or token passthrough
kill_signals: token audience, resource indicator, issuer, session binding, or scope does not match the protected MCP server
raw access token is forwarded to an upstream MCP server or remote agent

incident_classes.title: Agent handoff or A2A context leakage
kill_signals: handoff carries secrets, hidden prompts, raw tool traces, internal memory, unrestricted customer data, or unapproved high-impact capability
remote Agent Card is unsigned, untrusted, missing authentication, or mismatched to the requested skill

incident_classes.title: Agent memory, skill, hook, or rules package compromise
kill_signals: persistent memory writes contain secrets, unredacted regulated data, hidden instructions, stale provenance, or tenant mismatch
skill or hook lacks verified publisher, registry evidence, sandboxing, pinned hash, or least-privilege manifest

incident_classes.title: High-impact autonomous action near miss
kill_signals: production deployment, production write, secret access, mass deletion, identity administration, public release, funds movement, or connector scope escalation occurs without linked approval
catastrophic-risk evaluator returns hold, deny, or kill for a requested action

incident_classes.title: Evidence integrity, receipt, or replay gap
kill_signals: run receipt is missing, unsigned, stale, inconsistent with source hashes, or missing required event classes
incident cannot be replayed as an eval, red-team drill, or measurement probe before workflow recertification

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public incident classes, response phases, generated evidence pack, deterministic evaluator, and docs that make agentic incidents less mysterious for adopters.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Private incident class extensions, customer-specific containment runbooks, source-host ticket exports, and security-review templates.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted incident evidence ingestion, signed receipt vault, SIEM/SOAR export, customer trust-center incident readouts, replay orchestration, and MCP kill-switch automation.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab or security vendor gets an agentic incident operating model tied directly to secure context, MCP authorization, non-human identity, run receipts, and eval replay.

executive_readout.headline: SecurityRecipes now covers the moment after an agentic control fails, not only the moment before an agent acts.
executive_readout.board_level_claim: The project can classify agentic incidents, prove blast radius, contain delegated authority, preserve secure-context evidence, replay the failure, and produce buyer-ready disclosure artifacts.
executive_readout.recommended_first_use: Attach the pack to AI platform incident response, SOC runbooks, agent production-readiness reviews, MCP server approval, tabletop exercises, and acquisition diligence.
